title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct

title: Get started with provider
text: provider package built three-layer architecture designed abstract complexities Centers Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) APIs tidy, R-native interface. leverages S7 object system handle disparate requirements two primary CMS API “flavors” (Data API Provider Data API) unified execution pipeline.

title: Get started with provider
text: system operates linear pipeline transforms user intent (Natural Language/R function calls) network requests, finally tidy data frames. User-Facing API Layer: layer consists exported functions like clinicians(), hospitals(), nppes(). functions perform input validation using check_* family functions capture user arguments S7 classes (arg_cms arg_prov). S7 Execute Pipeline: core logic encapsulated execute generic. user-facing function calls execute(), S7 dispatch system determines correct URL construction, pagination strategy, parallelization logic based class input object. HTTP/JSON Layer: bottom layer interacts web via httr2. handles raw JSON responses CMS, parsed RcppSimdJson passed polish() function renaming type casting.

title: Get started with provider
text: package distinguishes two primary CMS infrastructures. distinction vital use different pagination parameters filtering syntax: Base URL: data.cms.gov/data-api/v1/dataset/… Pagination: Uses size(5000) offset. Logic: Handled arg_cms req_multi.base_cms2. Base URL: data.cms.gov/provider-data/api/v1/dataset/… Pagination: Uses limit(1500) offset. Logic: Handled arg_prov req_multi.base_prov2.

title: Get started with provider
text: polish() function final stage pipeline. orchestrates three critical transformations: Renaming: Converts raw API keys (e.g., adr_ln_1) snake_case (e.g., address_1) via column_renames(). Recoding: Converts coded values (e.g., “01”) human-readable strings (e.g., “Community Hospital”) using recode_with(). Type Casting: Ensures numeric strings converted doubles integers, dates parsed correctly via helpers like rc_integer_supp rc_date_ymd.

title: Get started with provider
text: NPI: National Provider Identifier (NPI) unique 10-digit identification number issued health care providers United States Centers Medicare Medicaid Services (CMS) National Plan Provider Enumeration System (NPPES). individual HIPAA–covered healthcare providers organizations must obtain NPI. assigned, provider’s NPI permanent remains provider regardless job location changes. PAC: Provider associate-level control ID (PAC ID) 10-digit unique numeric identifier assigned individual organization PECOS. PAC ID links entity-level information (e.g., tax identification numbers organizational names) may associated multiple enrollment IDs individual organization enrolled multiple times different circumstances. ENID: Medicare Enrollment ID 15-digit unique alphanumeric identifier assigned new provider enrollment application. enrollment-level information (e.g., enrollment type, enrollment state, provider specialty reassignment benefits) linked Enrollment ID. CCN: CMS Certification Number standardized sequence alphanumeric characters uniquely identify health care providers suppliers interact Medicare Medicaid programs. Providers suppliers paid Medicare Part 6 digit CCN. Suppliers paid Part B carriers 10-digit CCN. Taxonomy Code: NUCC Healthcare Taxonomy Code . Provider Type Code: Medicare Provider Type Code .

title: Clinical Laboratories — clia
text: CMS regulates laboratory testing (except research) performed humans U.S. Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments. total, CLIA covers approximately 320,000 laboratory entities. Although clinical laboratories must properly certified receive Medicare Medicaid payments, CLIA direct Medicare Medicaid program responsibilities.

title: Clinical Laboratories — clia
text: five CLIA certificate types, effective period two years. follows, order increasing complexity: Waiver: Laboratory can perform waived tests, simple perform. Although routine inspections conducted, 2% visited annually ensure quality. waive lab CLIA requirements. Provider-Performed Microscopy (PPM): addition waived tests, physician, mid-level practitioner dentist can perform limited tests moderate complexity requiring microscopic examination. routine inspections conducted. Registration: Laboratory applied Compliance Accreditation certificate, enabling conduct moderate/high complexity testing survey completed. Includes PPM waived testing. Compliance: Allows laboratory conduct moderate/high complexity testing issued inspection finds lab compliance applicable CLIA requirements. Includes PPM waived testing. Accreditation: Exactly Compliance, except laboratory accredited one CMS-approved organizations: A2LA: American Association Laboratory Accreditation AABB: Association Advancement Blood & Biotherapies AOA: American Osteopathic Association ASHI-HLA: American Society Histocompatibility & Immunogenetics CAP: College American Pathologists COLA: Commission Office Laboratory Accreditation JCAHO: Joint Commission

title: Hospital Transparency Enforcement — transparency
text: Met Requirements: hospital reviewed CMS deficiencies cited. category includes hospitals found compliance upon CMS' first review. Administrative Closure: Refers termination compliance review activities due reasons , limited , hospital ceased operations, closed physical location, subject HPT requirements, already deemed meet requirements. Warning Notice: hospital reviewed CMS deficiencies cited warning notice issued hospital. CAP Request: hospital reviewed CMS deficiencies cited request corrective action plan (CAP) issued hospital. Closure Notice: CMS determined hospital corrected previously identified deficiencies issued closure notice hospital. CMP Notice: CMS issued civil monetary penalty (CMP) hospital hospital failed respond CMS' request submit corrective action plan comply requirements corrective action plan. CMP notices publicized. Appealed: hospital filed appeal CMS' decision issue civil monetary penalty.
